re:sustain all about bringing together building automation & simulation in new way
for living, operational real estate to optimise performance

column2: without getting too technical let’s define what analytics, automation and simulation are in this context
so “analytics” (in the modern digital sense at least) is the systematic computational analysis of data or statistics
one of the first web analytics companies was called WebTrends which launched in 1993
this was a pivotal time – 1993 was the year of the "Eternal September"
before then the internet was basically a private club for universities
but in September '93, AOL opened the doors to the general public
the floodgates opened, millions of new users poured in, and the traffic never stopped after that
suddenly, for the first time, companies needed to know: "Who’re these people? And what are they clicking on?"
WebTrends basically invented the commercial "hit counter" (which was tracking this)
I could bore you to sobs talking about this now but happy to chat afterwards if anyone’s actually interested in what the Eternal September is all about
buildings are having their "1993 moment" right now
they’re so complex that it’s notoriously difficult to accurately measure their efficiency but that’s changing dramatically right now
so instead of taking the Schneider promotion, I left to join another startup, Demand Logic
(which  was founded back in 2007 as the original building analytics company in the UK)
